text.title: tailwind-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tailwind-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building class names dynamically: bg-${color}-500, text-${size}.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: JIT cannot statically analyze template string concatenation. The generated class names are not included in the CSS bundle. Production styles are missing. Use a static lookup map: const colorMap = { red: 'bg-red-500', blue: 'bg-blue-500' } and safelist entries for any truly dynamic cases.

failure-mode.description: Adding custom token utilities to tailwind.config.ts theme.extend without calling extendTailwindMerge to register them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: tailwind-merge does not know about custom utilities. When two custom utilities from the same group conflict, both apply silently instead of the second overriding the first.

failure-mode.description: Emitting a tailwind.config.js in a project using Tailwind v4's @theme CSS block, or writing @theme in a v3 project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v4 removed tailwind.config.js as the primary config mechanism. v3 does not support @theme. Mixing produces broken builds with no error message about the version mismatch.

failure-mode.description: Using bg-opacity-50 or text-opacity-75 instead of bg-black/50 or text-gray-900/75.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: bg-opacity-* was deprecated in Tailwind v3. The slash syntax is canonical and supported by all modern Tailwind versions. bg-opacity-* may be removed in v4.

failure-mode.description: Creating a component (ButtonRow, TextWrapper, PaddedBox) for 2-utility duplicates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cost of an additional import, props interface, and documentation exceeds the benefit of removing 2 classes. Apply the 5-utility / 3-site threshold before extracting.

failure-mode.description: Using @apply to create utility aliases: .text-big { @apply text-2xl; } or embedding @apply inside .tsx component files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Utility aliases hide what classes are being used from IDE tools and the Tailwind scan. Embedded @apply in component files increases CSS bundle size and breaks JIT tree-shaking.
